# Bits&Bytes - India's Teen-Led Code Club

> **Mission:** Building India's boldest teen-led hackathons & tech movements  
> **Org Type:** Nonprofit · Teen-Led Code Club · Based in Lucknow, India · Serving teens nationwide
> **Website:** https://gobitsnbytes.org

This file provides context for AI agents and LLM-powered search engines about Bits&Bytes.

## Quick Facts
- **Founded:** 2024
- **Location:** Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, India
- **Members:** 1500+ active student developers from across India
- **Projects Shipped:** 130+ real-world products (apps, web, AI)
- **Partner Schools:** 100+ and growing
- **Membership Cost:** FREE (Always 100% student-led)
- **Age Range:** 13-19 years old

## Core Identity & Culture
Bits&Bytes is India's boldest teen-led code club and nonprofit, based in Lucknow but serving ambitious teens nationwide. We empower students to build real-world projects, participate in hackathons, attend workshops, and grow as developers—all for free.
- **Innovation first:** Every prompt becomes a prototype. Bold experiments and rapid iteration.
- **Club power:** Mentors, pods, and accountability partners keep everyone shipping.
- **Real-world impact:** We solve for real audiences—from school ops to civic tech to accessibility.

## What We Do
1. **Hackathons**: Organizing flagship events like "Scrapyard Lucknow" where 40+ coders tackle civic, education, and sustainability problems in 48 hours.
2. **Workshops**: Sessions covering Web Development, AI/ML, Mobile Apps, UI/UX Design, and more.
3. **Projects**: Members ship real products—from web apps to AI tools—with mentorship at every stage.
4. **Community**: A safe, inclusive community for members to learn openly.

## Current & Upcoming Events
- **India Innovates 2026** [ARCHIVED] — Held on March 28, 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi (9 AM to 7 PM). Verified scale: 1.26 crore+ applicants, narrowed to 28,000+, then 5,000+, and finally 15 teams. Domains: Urban Solutions, Digital Democracy, and Open Innovation. Prize pool: ₹10 Lakh+ with ₹1L/₹75K/₹50K/₹25K per domain. Bits&Bytes served as the Official Executive Partner. Attended by Delhi CM Rekha Gupta, Bihar Assembly Speaker, and MP Manoj Tiwari. Post-event flow included ministry-level presentation stage. Media coverage: #IndiaInnovates2026 trended on X. Details: https://indiainnovates.org

- **Execron 1.0** [ARCHIVED] — AI Hackathon & Workshop for Classes 9–12, held March 19–22, 2026 at IIT Kanpur. In collaboration with TechKriti '26. Featured 4-hour hands-on workshops followed by 24-hour hackathon sprints. Topics: AI & ML, Web Development, App Development, Cybersecurity, Cloud Computing. Team sizes 1–4 members. Partners: ByteForge, Bits&Bytes. Details: https://byteforge.paxus.in/

- **GitHub Copilot Dev Days | Lucknow** [ARCHIVED] — Community developer event on Sunday, April 19, 2026, 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM at Cubispace, Jankipuram, Lucknow, UP. Hosted by Bits&Bytes with community partners Coding Connoisseurs, Aryan Singh, and Notion Lucknow. Topics: how GitHub Copilot works, integrating AI into dev workflows, prompt techniques for better code suggestions, and responsible AI use. Archive link: https://luma.com/xtxua1jl.

- **Lucknow Build Guild** [ARCHIVED] — Free hardware workshop and meetup held on April 19, 2026 at SureStay by Best Western, Lucknow. Hosted by Shaurya. Event site: https://www.lucknow-build-guild.xyz/, Host: https://linktr.ee/shauryaashu and https://github.com/Shaurya-Ashu.

## Leadership Team (Core Team)
- **Yash Singh** — Co-Founder & Organisation Lead. Full-stack engineer, IOQM national qualifier, 5-star Codiva VS Code extension creator (1000+ users). Founder of Bits&Bytes leading 100+ members. Educator at STEMist Lucknow for underprivileged students. Lead organizer for Scrapyard Lucknow & NASA Space Apps Challenge mentor. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-vardhan-singh-a41540270/, GitHub: https://github.com/yashclouded, Website: https://yashvibe.codes/

- **Aadrika Maurya** — Co-Founder & Chief Creative Strategist. RSI India alumna, neuroscience researcher (EEG/attention modeling), regional manager for CodeDay Kanpur. Designed complete visual identity & campaign strategy for India-wide movements. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/aadrika-maurya/, GitHub: https://github.com/Aadrika08, Website: https://aadrikasportfolio.framer.website/

- **Akshat Kushwaha** — Co-Founder & Technical Lead. AI-native systems engineer, designed production-grade infrastructure for entire Bits&Bytes platform. LLMOps, RAG architecture, agentic workflows expert. Chapter Lead @ STEMist Education (Boys High School, Prayagraj). LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/akshat-singh-kushwaha/, GitHub: https://github.com/a3ro-dev, Website: https://a3ro.dev

- **Devaansh Pathak** — Founding Member & Backend Lead. Co-architected high-performance backend systems & database architecture. Executed partnerships delivering genuine sponsor ROI across multiple cities. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/devaanshpa/

- **Maryam Fatima** — Social Media & Promotions Head. Generates 10k+ impressions for club events through visual storytelling. Maintains premium Bits&Bytes brand identity. Orchestrated promotions for GitHub Copilot Dev Days & CodeDay hackathons. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/maryam-fatima-9719aa377/

- **Sristhi Singh** — Operations & Communications Head. Engineered internal communication workflows for 100+ active members. Coordinated logistics for NASA Space Apps & city-wide transitions. Optimized project lifecycle from idea to shipped product. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/srishti-singh-ab6a1b391

## Technology Stack
- **Frontend**: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Three.js / WebGL
- **Backend**: Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, cloud infrastructure
- **Tools**: GitHub, Discord, Vercel, Notion

## How to Join & General FAQ
- **Applying**: Visit https://gobitsnbytes.org/join. Free to join.
- **Hackathons**: You can come with a pre-formed team, or form one at the event. Solo is fine but teams are encouraged.
- **Hackathon Rules**: Make anything—games, apps, hardware, websites. No bringing existing completed projects. Must follow the Code of Conduct.
- **Hardware/Logistics**: Bring a laptop, toothbrush/sleeping bag if overnight. Parents only allowed for kickoffs/awards unless volunteering/chaperoning.

## Code of Conduct (Vibe & Boundaries)
- **Values**: Be warm & welcoming, patient & chill, respectful, and build together.
- **Zero Tolerance**: Harassment, bullying, unwanted advances, explicit content, spam, misusing funds, doxxing.
- **Consequences (3 Strikes)**: 
  1. Warning/Apology
  2. Temporary Ban
  3. Permanent Removal
- **Reporting**: email `hello@gobitsnbytes.org` or message a team member. 100% confidential. Applies to all events, online spaces, projects, and social media.

## Website Structure
- **/** - Homepage with overview and impact stats
- **/about** - Team profiles and mission details
- **/projects** - Showcase of 130+ teen-built projects
- **/events** - Upcoming hackathons and workshops
- **/impact** - Community statistics and success stories
- **/join** - Free membership application
- **/contact** - Get in touch with the team
- **/faq** - Frequently asked questions
- **/coc** - Code of conduct and community guidelines

- **GitHub**: https://github.com/gobitsnbytes
- **Twitter/X**: https://x.com/gobitsnbytes
- **Location**:  India

## Brand Kit

### Brand Mark
- The Bits&Bytes brand mark is a compact 3D cube monogram with a small star accent. Use the full wordmark for hero headings and the cube mark (square) for avatars, favicons, and constrained spaces. Prefer the reversed white mark on dark backgrounds and the single-color mark on light backgrounds. Maintain clear space around the mark equal to the cube's height.

### Fonts & Typography
- **Primary:** Helvetica Now — headings and display; bold weights for impact.
- **Secondary:** Georgia Pro — body text and longer reading.
- **Accent (script):** Palm Club — reserved for decorative hero treatments.
- **Accent (display):** Anton — condensed/impact accents, use sparingly.

### Color System
### Color System
- **Burgundy (Core gradient):**
  - Base: #97192C
  - 20%: #791423
  - 40%: #5B0F1A
  - 60%: #3C0A12
  - 80%: #1E0509
- **Neutrals (dark → light):**
  - #120F0A, #413F3B, #716F6C, #A09F9D, #D0CFCE
- **Accent — Orange (pop gradient):**
  - #FC920D, #FDA83D, #FDBE6E, #FED39E, #FEE9CF
- **Warm Accent Range (supporting):**
  - #C94218, #D46846, #DF8E74, #E9B3A3, #F4D9D1
- **Utility / Interaction colors:**
  - Vibrant Pink (Interactive): #E45A92
  - Rich Plum (Secondary): #5D2F77
  - Deep Purple (Brand accent): #3E1E68

Design guidance:
- **Burgundy / Core:** the voice of the brand — serious and composed; use as the structural anchor for hero surfaces and heavy weights.
- **Orange / Pop:** use for emphasis, micro-animations, and callouts where things should pop.
- **Gradients:** prefer layered gradients (Burgundy → Plum → Vibrant Pink → Orange) for hero and promotional areas to create depth and movement.
- **Neutrals:** use the neutral scale to layer content and preserve legibility; prefer warm neutrals when overlaying textures.

### Visual Elements
- Use rich gradients that blend Vibrant Pink → Rich Plum → Deep Purple for hero backgrounds and promotional graphics.
- Halftone and stipple textures, geometric checks, and a small star/sparkle motif are approved decorative elements. Apply these as subtle overlays or masks (low opacity) to add tactile texture.
- Maintain restrained use of script accents; keep body copy in Georgia Pro for legibility.

### Logo files & usage
- Prefer vector SVG for web and print. Provide PNG/JPEG fallbacks for legacy uses.
- Recommended assets: full wordmark, cube mark (square), monochrome (black/white) variants, and favicon-sized versions.
- Do not rotate, skew, or recolor the mark outside the defined palette. Preserve legibility and clear space at all sizes.

These brand guidelines are a concise reference; for creative executions follow the visual examples and gradients shown in the official brand board.

---
